method.name: llm_reasoned
method.description: Compute statistical pairwise evidence, then ask an LLM to reason over stop-profile, representative route-image, and fleet-metadata evidence to produce final pairwise total_correlation values.

route_similarity.description: For each truck, choose the representative image from each route cluster, compare representative route-ink vectors by cosine similarity, and average pairwise similarities weighted by cluster shares.

method.global_rationale: The correlations were derived from a composite of existing statistics pertaining to operational similarities. Feature analysis on stop patterns, routing, and fleet metadata contributed significantly to final correlations.
risks: Potential undervaluation of unique operational characteristics that may have been overridden by general feature patterns.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: High similarity due to matching purposes, significant miles similarity, and very high stop profile correlation.

llm_feature_interpretation.stop_profile_correlation: Both vehicles exhibit very similar stopping patterns.
llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Moderate shared routes aligning with purpose.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Aged similarly and both utilized for efficient duties.

pairwise_argument_correlations.llm_rationale: Both trucks serve the same purpose and possess a high correlation in stopping and routing patterns.

llm_feature_interpretation.route_similarity: Similar route patterns among shared clusters.
llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Similar age and suitable for similar tasks.
